summary: Fix login case-insensitivity by updating storage lookup logic and resolve delayed error messages by moving the Toaster component to the application root.

plan.title: Make user lookup case-insensitive in DatabaseStorage
plan.description: Update `getUserByUsername` in `server/databaseStorage.ts` to use `sql` with `LOWER()` for a case-insensitive match.

plan.title: Make user lookup case-insensitive in FileStorage
plan.description: Update `getUserByUsername` in `server/fileStorage.ts` to use `toLowerCase()` when comparing usernames.

plan.title: Globalize Toaster and TooltipProvider in App.tsx
plan.description: Move `Toaster` and `TooltipProvider` in `client/src/App.tsx` from the conditional `/` route to the root of the `App` component, ensuring they are always mounted.

acceptance_criteria.description: In `server/databaseStorage.ts`, `getUserByUsername` uses `sql` expression with `LOWER()` on both sides of the comparison.
In `client/src/App.tsx`, `Toaster` and `TooltipProvider` are rendered outside of the `Switch` or conditional route logic.
Logging in with 'USER', 'user', or 'User' succeeds for a user registered as 'user'.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Manual test: Register a user and attempt login with different cases.

acceptance_criteria.description: Login error messages (toasts) appear immediately on the AuthPage.
acceptance_criteria.how_to_verify: Manual test: Enter wrong password on login page and verify toast visibility.

risks: If existing users have identical usernames differing only by case, `getUserByUsername` will return the first match found by the database.
Moving `Toaster` might conflict with other layout elements if not placed correctly in the DOM hierarchy.
